title: Aluminum rose to 3,503.05 USD/T on May 8, 2026
summary: Aluminum prices rose to 3,503.05 USD/T, up 0.57% from the previous day. Over the past month, aluminum's price has risen 1.64%, and is up 44.69% year-over-year. The rebound is attributed to expectations of prolonged supply disruptions from the Middle East.

title: Aluminum Price at 3,599.85 - Up 6.73% from Last Month
summary: Aluminum prices are currently at 3,599.85, up 6.73% from last month (3,372.95) and up 35.42% compared to one year ago (2,658.29). This reflects significant upward momentum in the aluminum market.

title: Aluminium Futures at 3,503.05 - Technical Analysis
summary: Aluminium futures are trading at 3,503.05 with a previous close of 3,483.23. Technical analysis suggests aluminum is approaching key support zones with potential for bullish rebound if buyers regain control.

title: Canada's HUGE Aluminum Discovery Just ROCKED Global Supply
summary: A massive alumina discovery in Saskatchewan, known as the Thor project, has triggered a strategic scramble among industrial powers. This discovery is being positioned as a potential game-changer for North America's supply chain, manufacturing strength, and critical minerals strategy.

title: 4 MIN AGO: Canada Uncovers Massive Aluminum Find
summary: Canada has uncovered a massive alumina/aluminum-related discovery in Saskatchewan that could change the future of the global aluminum market. The discovery is being analyzed for its implications on global aluminum supply, North American manufacturing strength, and critical minerals strategy amid rising trade tensions and supply chain pressures.

title: Global Aluminium Production Decline in Q1 2026
summary: Global aluminium production slipped from 18.6 million tonnes in Q4 2025 to 18.3 million tonnes in Q1 2026, representing a 1.6 percent decline. This data reflects current market conditions in the aluminum production sector.

title: Hydro Q1: Upstream production rises as North American extrusion demand declines
summary: Hydro's Q1 results show higher prices supporting margins despite weaker North American extrusion demand. Upstream production increased during the quarter.

title: Steel fell to 3244 CNY/T on May 8, 2026
summary: Steel prices declined 0.22% to 3,244 CNY/T, though prices have risen 5.63% over the past month and 6.40% year-over-year. Steel rebar futures surged to around CNY 3,250 per ton, reaching their strongest level since August 2025, supported by robust buying in mainland China and a continued seven-week drawdown in steel inventories.

title: India's NMDC raises iron ore prices in May
summary: Indian state-controlled mining company NMDC raised iron ore prices for the fourth consecutive month in May, setting 10-40mm Baila 65.5% lump ore at 5,500 rupees/t ($58/t) and 64% Baila fines at Rs4,700/t. The price increases came despite domestic steel prices facing pressure from slow demand.

title: US Structural Steel Pricing Update
summary: Current structural steel pricing stands at $53.06 cwt as of May 7, 2026, reflecting recent market movements in the US steel sector.

title: Chilean miner Codelco's El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco announced that its El Teniente smelter is set to restart on Thursday following a collapse. This restart is expected to impact copper supply dynamics in the market.

title: Copper futures remain above $5.95 per pound supported by China demand
summary: Copper futures remained above $5.95 per pound on Friday after rebounding in the previous session. The market was supported by signs of improving demand in China despite ongoing headwinds from the Middle East conflict.

title: Copper rises to 6.23 USD/Lbs, up 1.62% on May 8
summary: Copper rose to 6.23 USD/Lbs on May 8, 2026, up 1.62% from the previous day. Over the past month, copper's price has risen 8.33%, and is up 35.18% compared to the same period last year.

title: Record copper price signals accelerating race for supplies
summary: Copper prices soared to records above $13,000 a metric ton, driven by fears of shortages and geopolitical turmoil in Venezuela. Strong demand from data centers for AI and electric vehicles fueled a 40% price increase last year. Citi analysts estimate a 308,000 ton deficit in refined copper production this year.

title: Copper Market News & Forecasts
summary: Glencore's African copper assets surged 68% year-on-year to 67,900 tons. Major companies are securing long-term supply through offtake deals and acquisitions, including Mitsui's 40% stake in Argentina's Josemaria deposit and Fortescue's acquisition of Peru's Cañariaco project.

title: Copper price highest in over a year on Grasberg disruption, Fed outlook
summary: Copper prices climbed above $10,500 per tonne on the London Metal Exchange, driven by global supply disruptions and expected US interest rate cuts. Freeport-McMoRan declared force majeure at its Grasberg mine in Indonesia, adding to supply challenges across South America and Africa.

title: Copper Markets in Crisis After a Year of Disruptions
summary: Major accidents at Grasberg, El Teniente, and Kakula mines have eliminated hundreds of thousands of tons of expected copper output in 2025. Grasberg's output is projected 35% lower than forecast, with full recovery not expected until 2027. Nearly 600,000 tons of contained copper will be lost between September 2025 and end of 2026 due to the Grasberg disruption alone.

title: Freeport delays Grasberg full restart to early 2028
summary: Freeport now targets 65% capacity in the second half of 2026, 80% by mid-2027, and near full capacity by the end of 2027. This represents a significant delay in the recovery timeline for one of the world's largest copper mines.

title: New copper discoveries becoming pressing matter with production from top producer drying up
summary: S&P Global predicts copper supply will lag demand starting in 2025, with global mine production dropping from 21.5Mt to 15.9Mt by 2030. Chile's copper output tumbled 15% month-on-month and 7.5% year-on-year, indicating a pending supply crunch. The industry needs to spend over $100 billion to address an estimated 4.7Mt deficit by 2030.

title: Technology Minerals unlocks new innovation and revenue stream with copper and aluminium recovery
summary: Technology Minerals has introduced a new innovation for recovering copper and aluminium, creating a fresh revenue stream. This development focuses on battery recycling and sustainable mineral extraction. The initiative highlights advancements in technology for resource recovery.

title: Rewiring a sustainable, clean energy future with copper
summary: Copper's role in efficiently transmitting electricity is crucial for minimizing energy losses in the transition to a net-zero future. The article discusses copper as a critical mineral for advanced technologies like EVs and digital infrastructure. Innovations in copper use are key to sustainable energy systems.

title: Innovations to close the copper supply-demand gap
summary: New technologies and favorable policies are essential to bridge the looming copper supply-demand gap amid declining ore grades. Copper remains vital since the Bronze Age for human development. The focus is on innovative solutions for sustainable supply.

title: Nickel Futures Surge Near 2-Year High
summary: Nickel futures traded above $19,500 per tonne, nearing a two-year high, driven by supply disruptions and tightening raw material availability. Mining quota cuts in Indonesia have constrained ore supply, while a sulfur shortage linked to Strait of Hormuz disruptions has raised processing costs for nickel intermediates.

title: Nickel Prices Pull Back from Recent Rally as Profit-Taking Emerges
summary: Nickel fell to 18,878 USD/T on May 8, down 1.24% from the previous day, as traders locked in gains following a sharp rally. The pullback reflects profit-taking rather than deterioration in fundamentals, with broader industrial metals showing mixed price action.

title: Nickel Futures Price Today
summary: Current nickel futures price is 18,906.50, down from previous close of 19,083.25. Market outlook remains bearish according to UBS warnings of persistent surplus through 2026 despite recent production cuts.

title: Nornickel says nickel output flat year-on-year in first quarter
summary: Russia's Nornickel, the world's largest palladium producer and major refined nickel producer, reported flat nickel output of 42,000 metric tons in Q1 compared to the same period last year. The company maintained its full-year nickel output forecast between 193,000 and 203,000 metric tons.

title: Indonesia on track to become largest global producer of refined nickel
summary: Indonesia is positioned to become the world's largest refined nickel producer, a critical mineral for lithium-ion batteries and steel production. However, analysis reveals that over 75% of the country's refining capacity is controlled by Chinese stakeholders, many with ties to the CCP.

title: How the Nickel Industry Is Rebuilding Supply Outside Indonesia
summary: Supply concentration in nickel, particularly in Indonesia, is driving price increases and strengthening investment cases for large-scale sulphide projects outside the country. The industry is actively diversifying production capacity away from Indonesia.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Indonesia has ordered PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world’s largest nickel mine, to significantly cut its ore production quota from 42 million tons in 2025 to 12 million tons in 2026, as part of efforts to stabilize global nickel prices and manage supply. The move highlights growing tensions between rapid nickel expansion and sustainable resource management in Indonesia’s critical minerals sector.

title: Mining with Integrity to Advance Sustainable Nickel Downstreaming
summary: Indonesia is pushing for more sustainable nickel downstreaming by emphasizing mining integrity, emissions reductions, and lower carbon intensity in nickel products, with targets to cut emissions by 33% by 2030 and reduce carbon intensity by up to 50%. The article underscores how Indonesia’s 2020 nickel ore export ban has reshaped its strategy toward value-added, cleaner nickel production.

title: New Technology Set to Revolutionize Nickel Pollution Cleanup
summary: A new technology using olivine, a naturally occurring mineral, is being developed to clean up nickel-related pollution and permanently store carbon dioxide, offering a dual solution for environmental remediation and climate mitigation. The approach could transform how the nickel industry manages its environmental footprint, especially in mining and processing regions.

title: Tenaris Warns That The Middle East Conflict Will Affect Second-Quarter Sales
summary: Tenaris, an Italian steel pipe manufacturer, warned that its second-quarter sales will be impacted by lower shipments in the Middle East due to the ongoing conflict and the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz from the U.S. and Israel war against Iran. The company anticipates higher logistic costs affecting margins, but expects sales and margins to recover in the second half of the year if the waterway reopens. First-quarter sales rose 6% to $3.1 billion, driven by strong North American growth offsetting declines in Asia Pacific and the Middle East.

title: Newsroom | Tenaris
summary: Tenaris's official newsroom announces a definitive agreement to acquire 100% of the share capital of a U.S.-based company from GLGH Steel, LLC. The page provides updates on company projects, events, products, and more. Users can subscribe to the newsletter for the latest Tenaris news.
